markdownRemark.excerpt: This is something I’m still struggling to understand. Why does Teams suck, this much? Even though Microsoft isn’t neither short of money nor talent to make it…
markdownRemark.html:
This is something I’m still struggling to understand. Why does Teams suck, this much? Even though Microsoft isn’t neither short of money nor talent to make it suck less. Is their codebase so large that they just couldn’t rewrite some parts of it? Do they just not care? Is it too late? Maybe the people at Microsoft just don’t realize it even sucks. I don’t know. When Steve Jobs visited Xerox to steal their ideas, the year 1979, that’s when Teams would’ve been a cool software to use. When you think about it, it’s even kind of sad, for a product that was born out of “Ok, this will be our Slack competitor”. Teams’ sole reason of existence is to be a competitor for a far better and superior product, knowing it will never reach that level. I’d definitely not want to be in Teams’ shoes. Now I started feeling bad for some piece of software that has no feelings. Tragic.

This software is really soulless. It basically sucks fun and joy out of the person who has the unfortunate luck to use it. The user interface, combined and hand in hand with its terrible user experience, is almost, intentionally, designed in a way to be annoying. It is not amateur bad, it is professional evil. It is ugly and depressing. It is like when Toby entered Michael Scott’s room and started talking with his grouchy voice. It is like when Soviet Union architects tried to build a communication app for work and it happened to be Teams. Microsoft might as well just be allergic to good UI/UX. I don’t know.

The main issue is usability, how smooth and fast you can not message and navigate around, how the UI moves in totally unpredictable ways. You open an image? The best media viewer of all time. I especially like that subtle gap before an image loads. It’s beautiful. Big part of workplace communication takes place in channels. Where many people are a part of a chat. On Teams, these channels are in a different tab. You have to go back and forth in between (chats and channels) to be active in more than one conversation, which probably is the most of the time. But there is a big separation between the “people” and “channel” chats. That’s one of the worst design flaws it has. It’s making it incredibly difficult to keep track of activity and the many conversations going on at the same time. It’s fucking heavy lifting. And, have you tried scrolling up in a chat to see older messages? Now that’s next level sucking.

Terrible performance, terrible user experience and interface, flawed design. Some people compare it’s terribleness to that of WebEx or Skype Business. I don’t know much about them but I’ve seen hell. And that’s Teams. I do not have faith that it will ever be a good chatting platform for work. It’s a curse and also a blessing to be a Microsoft product. It’s a curse because it will never be Microsoft’s top priority to make a good chat platform. Will never get full focus. And it’s blessing because many companies will use it because, well, it’s a Microsoft product.

This post is not meant to be giving constructive feedback. It’s not my intention at this moment. All of this is personal opinion and experience. If you’re someone who works on Teams and reads this post, please do not feel bad. It’s okay.

frontmatter.title: Developer vs Engineer, a Fairy Tale
